---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```
```{r}

# Create sample plant_joined data with spring and autumn seasons
set.seed(123)
plant_joined <- data.frame(
  season.x = rep(c("spring", "autumn"), each = 100),
  nitrogen_level = c(
    rnorm(100, mean = 3.5, sd = 0.8),  # Spring
    rnorm(100, mean = 2.8, sd = 0.7)   # Autumn
  ),
  egg_count = c(
    rpois(100, lambda = 45),   # Spring
    rpois(100, lambda = 30)    # Autumn
  ),
  larvae_count = c(
    rpois(100, lambda = 38),   # Spring
    rpois(100, lambda = 25)    # Autumn
  ),
  occupied = sample(c(0, 1), 200, replace = TRUE),
  temperature_ambient = rnorm(200, mean = 20, sd = 5),
  temperature_soil = rnorm(200, mean = 18, sd = 4)
)

ui <- page_fillable(
  theme = bs_theme(
    bg = "#e8f4f8",
    fg = "#2c3e50",
    primary = "#FF6B9D",
    "navbar-bg" = "#4A90E2"
  ),
  
  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
        background-attachment: fixed;
      }
      .card {
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95) !important;
        border-radius: 15px !important;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) !important;
      }
      .card-header {
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #FF6B9D 0%, #C06C84 100%) !important;
        color: white !important;
        font-weight: bold !important;
        border-radius: 15px 15px 0 0 !important;
        padding: 15px !important;
      }
      h2 {
        color: white !important;
        text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
        margin-bottom: 20px;
      }
      .butterfly-emoji {
        font-size: 3em;
        text-align: center;
        margin: 10px 0;
      }
    "))
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "padding: 20px;",
    h2("🦋 Plant Data Dashboard 🦋", style = "text-align: center;"),
    
    layout_columns(
      col_widths = c(3, 9),
      
      # Sidebar with controls
      card(
        card_header("Data Controls"),
        div(class = "butterfly-emoji", "🌱"),
        
        sliderInput(
          "nitrogen_range",
          "Nitrogen Level Range (mg/L):",
          min = 0,
          max = 8,
          value = c(0, 8),
          step = 0.1
        ),
        
        checkboxGroupInput(
          "seasons",
          "Select Seasons:",
          choices = c("Spring" = "spring", "Autumn" = "autumn"),
          selected = c("spring", "autumn")
        ),
        
        selectInput(
          "plot_type",
          "Visualization Type:",
          choices = c(
            "Nitrogen vs Eggs" = "eggs",
            "Nitrogen vs Larvae" = "larvae",
            "Eggs vs Larvae" = "comparison"
          )
        )
      ),
      
      # Main content area
      div(
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(12),
          
          card(
            card_header("Main Statistics"),
            plotOutput("main_plot", height = "400px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count Distribution"),
            plotOutput("egg_distribution", height = "300px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Larvae Count Distribution"),
            plotOutput("larvae_distribution", height = "300px")
          )
        ),
        
        card(
          card_header("Summary Statistics"),
          tableOutput("summary_table")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  # Reactive filtered data
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    plant_joined %>%
      filter(
        season.x %in% input$seasons,
        nitrogen_level >= input$nitrogen_range[1],
        nitrogen_level <= input$nitrogen_range[2]
      )
  })
  
  # Season color palette
  season_colors <- c(
    "spring" = "#90EE90",
    "autumn" = "#D2691E"
  )
  
  # Main plot
  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
                 label = "No data available for selected filters",
                 size = 6, color = "gray50") +
        theme_void()
    } else if (input$plot_type == "eggs") {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = egg_count, color = season.x)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
        labs(
          title = "Effect of Nitrogen Levels on Egg Count",
          x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
          y = "Egg Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    } else if (input$plot_type == "larvae") {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
        labs(
          title = "Effect of Nitrogen Levels on Larvae Count",
          x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, y = larvae_count, color = season.x)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
        labs(
          title = "Relationship Between Egg Count and Larvae Count",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })
  
  # Egg distribution
  output$egg_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        x = "Egg Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  })
  
  # Larvae distribution
  output$larvae_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = larvae_count, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors, labels = c("Autumn", "Spring")) +
      labs(
        x = "Larvae Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  })
  
  # Summary table
  output$summary_table <- renderTable({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "No data available"))
    
    data %>%
      group_by(season.x) %>%
      summarise(
        `Avg Nitrogen (mg/L)` = round(mean(nitrogen_level), 2),
        `Avg Egg Count` = round(mean(egg_count), 1),
        `Avg Larvae Count` = round(mean(larvae_count), 1),
        `Total Observations` = n()
      ) %>%
      rename(Season = season.x)
  }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, bordered = TRUE)
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create sample data for egg laying analysis
set.seed(123)
```

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create sample butterfly breeding data
set.seed(123)
butterfly_data <- data.frame(
  season = rep(c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter"), each = 50),
  nitrogen_level = c(
    rnorm(50, mean = 3.5, sd = 0.8),  # Spring
    rnorm(50, mean = 4.2, sd = 0.9),  # Summer
    rnorm(50, mean = 2.8, sd = 0.7),  # Autumn
    rnorm(50, mean = 2.0, sd = 0.6)   # Winter
  ),
  egg_count = c(
    rpois(50, lambda = 45),   # Spring
    rpois(50, lambda = 65),   # Summer
    rpois(50, lambda = 30),   # Autumn
    rpois(50, lambda = 15)    # Winter
  ),
  larvae_count = c(
    rpois(50, lambda = 38),   # Spring
    rpois(50, lambda = 55),   # Summer
    rpois(50, lambda = 25),   # Autumn
    rpois(50, lambda = 12)    # Winter
  )
)

ui <- page_fillable(
  theme = bs_theme(
    bg = "#e8f4f8",
    fg = "#2c3e50",
    primary = "#FF6B9D",
    "navbar-bg" = "#4A90E2"
  ),
  
  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body {
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
        background-attachment: fixed;
      }
      .card {
        background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95) !important;
        border-radius: 15px !important;
        box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1) !important;
      }
      .card-header {
        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #FF6B9D 0%, #C06C84 100%) !important;
        color: white !important;
        font-weight: bold !important;
        border-radius: 15px 15px 0 0 !important;
        padding: 15px !important;
      }
      h2 {
        color: white !important;
        text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
        margin-bottom: 20px;
      }
      .butterfly-emoji {
        font-size: 3em;
        text-align: center;
        margin: 10px 0;
      }
    "))
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "padding: 20px;",
    h2("🦋 Butterfly Breeder Dashboard 🦋", style = "text-align: center;"),
    
    layout_columns(
      col_widths = c(3, 9),
      
      # Sidebar with controls
      card(
        card_header("Breeding Controls"),
        div(class = "butterfly-emoji", "🦋"),
        
        sliderInput(
          "nitrogen_range",
          "Nitrogen Level Range (mg/L):",
          min = 0,
          max = 8,
          value = c(0, 8),
          step = 0.1
        ),
        
        checkboxGroupInput(
          "seasons",
          "Select Seasons:",
          choices = c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter"),
          selected = c("Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter")
        ),
        
        selectInput(
          "plot_type",
          "Visualization Type:",
          choices = c(
            "Nitrogen vs Eggs" = "eggs",
            "Nitrogen vs Larvae" = "larvae",
            "Eggs vs Larvae" = "comparison"
          )
        )
      ),
      
      # Main content area
      div(
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(12),
          
          card(
            card_header("Breeding Statistics"),
            plotOutput("main_plot", height = "400px")
          )
        ),
        
        layout_columns(
          col_widths = c(6, 6),
          
          card(
            card_header("Egg Count Distribution"),
            plotOutput("egg_distribution", height = "300px")
          ),
          
          card(
            card_header("Larvae Count Distribution"),
            plotOutput("larvae_distribution", height = "300px")
          )
        ),
        
        card(
          card_header("Summary Statistics"),
          tableOutput("summary_table")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  # Reactive filtered data
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    butterfly_data %>%
      filter(
        season %in% input$seasons,
        nitrogen_level >= input$nitrogen_range[1],
        nitrogen_level <= input$nitrogen_range[2]
      )
  })
  
  # Season color palette
  season_colors <- c(
    "Spring" = "#90EE90",
    "Summer" = "#FFD700",
    "Autumn" = "#D2691E",
    "Winter" = "#87CEEB"
  )
  
  # Main plot
  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) {
      ggplot() +
        annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5, 
                 label = "No data available for selected filters",
                 size = 6, color = "gray50") +
        theme_void()
    } else if (input$plot_type == "eggs") {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = egg_count, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Effect of Nitrogen Levels on Egg Count",
          x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
          y = "Egg Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    } else if (input$plot_type == "larvae") {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = nitrogen_level, y = larvae_count, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Effect of Nitrogen Levels on Larvae Count",
          x = "Nitrogen Level (mg/L)",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    } else {
      ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, y = larvae_count, color = season)) +
        geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
        scale_color_manual(values = season_colors) +
        labs(
          title = "Relationship Between Egg Count and Larvae Count",
          x = "Egg Count",
          y = "Larvae Count",
          color = "Season"
        ) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
        theme(
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "bottom"
        )
    }
  })
  
  # Egg distribution
  output$egg_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = egg_count, fill = season)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors) +
      labs(
        x = "Egg Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  })
  
  # Larvae distribution
  output$larvae_distribution <- renderPlot({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    ggplot(data, aes(x = larvae_count, fill = season)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 20, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = season_colors) +
      labs(
        x = "Larvae Count",
        y = "Frequency",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  })
  
  # Summary table
  output$summary_table <- renderTable({
    data <- filtered_data()
    
    if (nrow(data) == 0) return(data.frame(Message = "No data available"))
    
    data %>%
      group_by(season) %>%
      summarise(
        `Avg Nitrogen (mg/L)` = round(mean(nitrogen_level), 2),
        `Avg Egg Count` = round(mean(egg_count), 1),
        `Avg Larvae Count` = round(mean(larvae_count), 1),
        `Total Observations` = n()
      )
  }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, bordered = TRUE)
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

```

```{r}
library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create sample data that matches your structure
set.seed(123)
plant_joined <- data.frame(
  occupied = sample(c(0, 1), 200, replace = TRUE),
  temperature_ambient = rnorm(200, mean = 20, sd = 5),
  temperature_soil = rnorm(200, mean = 18, sd = 4),
  season.x = sample(c("autumn", "spring"), 200, replace = TRUE)
)

ui <- page_sidebar(
  title = "Plant Data by Season",
  sidebar = sidebar(
    radioButtons(
      "season",
      "Select Season:",
      choices = c("Autumn" = "autumn", "Spring" = "spring"),
      selected = "autumn"
    )
  ),
  layout_columns(
    card(
      card_header("Occupancy Distribution"),
      plotOutput("occupancy_plot")
    ),
    card(
      card_header("Soil Temperature Density"),
      plotOutput("temp_soil_plot")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$occupancy_plot <- renderPlot({
    plant_joined %>% 
      filter(season.x == input$season) %>%
      mutate(occupied = factor(occupied)) %>% 
      ggplot(aes(x = occupied)) +
      geom_bar(aes(fill = season.x), color = "black", position = "dodge") +
      scale_fill_manual(
        values = c("autumn" = "#D2691E", "spring" = "#90EE90")
      ) +
      labs(
        x = "Occupied",
        y = "Count",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(text = element_text(size = 14))
  })
  
  output$temp_soil_plot <- renderPlot({
    plant_joined %>% 
      filter(season.x == input$season) %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = temperature_soil, color = season.x, fill = season.x)) +
      geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
      scale_color_manual(
        values = c("autumn" = "#D2691E", "spring" = "#90EE90")
      ) +
      scale_fill_manual(
        values = c("autumn" = "#D2691E", "spring" = "#90EE90")
      ) +
      labs(
        x = "Soil Temperature",
        y = "Density",
        color = "Season",
        fill = "Season"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(text = element_text(size = 14))
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```


```{r}
egg_laying 

ui <- page_fillable(
  title = "Larvae Count Correlation Analysis",
  card(
    card_header("Correlation Heatmap: Factors Affecting Larvae Counts"),
    plotOutput("correlation_heatmap", height = "600px")
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$correlation_heatmap <- renderPlot({
    # Calculate correlation matrix
    cor_matrix <- cor(egg_laying, use = "complete.obs")
    
    # Convert to long format using tidyr instead of reshape2
    melted_cor <- as.data.frame(cor_matrix) %>%
      mutate(Var1 = rownames(.)) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -Var1, names_to = "Var2", values_to = "value")
    
    # Create heatmap
    ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white") +
      geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), size = 4) +
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#0571b0",
        mid = "white",
        high = "#ca0020",
        midpoint = 0,
        limits = c(-1, 1),
        name = "Correlation"
      ) +
      labs(
        title = "Correlation Matrix: Factors Affecting Larvae Counts",
        x = "",
        y = ""
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10)
      ) +
      coord_fixed()
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
